title: diospyros domingensis subsp. oxycarpa
intro: Diospyros domingensis subsp. oxycarpa, also known as the Cuban persimmon, is a plant species belonging to the family Ebenaceae. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Cuba, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds. Diospyros domingensis subsp. oxycarpa is often used to prepare herbal infusions, teas, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
